seoTitle: Port Willunga vs Porter Lagoon — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Port Willunga and Porter Lagoon. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Port Willunga vs Porter Lagoon: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Port Willunga and Porter Lagoon.
heading: Port Willunga vs Porter Lagoon — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Port Willunga, South Australia and Porter Lagoon, South Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Port Willunga has a lower overall climate risk score (31/100) compared to Porter Lagoon (41/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Port Willunga or Porter Lagoon safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Port Willunga has a flood risk score of 22/100, while Porter Lagoon scores 56/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Port Willunga scores 35/100 for bushfire risk. Porter Lagoon scores 47/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Port Willunga or Porter Lagoon?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Port Willunga has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.63% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
